raw_consumer_statement: I filed a dispute on my credit card for a moving company after they forced me to pay an extra fee on my credit card once they arrived for an interstate move, after initial estimates were given. They then damaged a list of items and refused to return emails or phone calls to cover the damaged goods. I provided all of this information to Citi card who then sent me a letter saying the company deserved to be paid and the dispute was closed. This was without any information as to the response of the merchant or any response to the numerous documents I provided. The letter has no explanation of how this decision was made, nor any information on how to submit an appeal or rebuttal. It seems Citi card does not have the customer 's best interest when they don't even provide any explanation, just a quick denial with no details.
